Description
Position at Androscoggin Animal Hospital

Associate Veterinarian 
Androscoggin Animal Hospital (Topsham, ME)
If you’re looking for a veterinary home where teamwork actually means something, where your input is valued, and where lunch breaks really do happen — Androscoggin Animal Hospital might be the perfect fit.
We’re an AAHA-accredited, seven-doctor practice (but we'd like to be fully rounded with eight or nine!) proudly serving the pets of Southern Maine since 1985. Around here, excellence isn’t just a buzzword — it’s the standard. Our team is known for delivering top-tier medicine (AAHA sets the bar high!), but also for being downright good humans who take care of each other. Many of our staff members have been with us for years — even decades — and that longevity says it all.
What We’re Looking For
We’re open to veterinarians of all experience levels, but we’d especially love to add someone who’s confident in (or excited to learn more about) exotic pet care — from bunnies to bearded dragons. You’ll have a strong, experienced support team behind you so you can focus on what you do best: practicing great medicine and connecting with clients.
What Makes Us Different
AAHA-accredited means we meet over 900 standards of veterinary excellence — and we’re proud of it.
No overnights, no on-call — we believe rest is part of doing your best work.
Half-day Saturdays (shared rotation) so you can start your weekend early.
Midday breaks built into the schedule — time to catch up on notes, treatments, or just breathe. Some of our staff like to go for a walk down the beautiful country road, work out (in our on-site exercise room!), or just take the time to breathe
A full, skilled support staff that keeps things running smoothly (and laughs often).
A culture that’s supportive, positive, and genuinely fun — our team works hard and has each other’s backs.
About Life in Topsham
Topsham sits right on the Androscoggin River, just across from Brunswick and minutes from Freeport (hello, L.L. Bean!). On your days off, you can kayak, hike, ski, or grab some of Maine’s best seafood — all without straying far from home. Boston, the White Mountains, and even Montreal are just a few hours away if you’re craving a bigger adventure.
If you’re looking for a team that values medicine and morale, we’d love to hear from you. Come see why we were voted “Best of the Best Veterinarian” in Downeast Dog News for multiple years, and why our staff sticks around — not because they have to, but because they want to.

The greater Austin area sits on the edge of "The Texas Hill Country,” and is a great destination for outdoor enthusiasts due to the hiking, mountain biking, climbing, road cycling, and water activities available at our many regional lakes. Many rivers and lakes in the area host water-based sports such as standup paddleboard, kayaking, canoeing, water skiing, tubing, wakeboarding, fishing, and rowing. The culinary and nightlife activities of The Hill Country have a little something for everyone and continue to grow. For more information about Firehouse and our Veterinarian position, visit our website, https://firehouse.vet/careers. We hope to see you soon!

Description
Position at Four Paws Veterinary Center

Join Our Thriving Team at Four Paws Veterinary Center, Dublin, CA
 
Are you a dedicated veterinarian looking to thrive in a collaborative environment? Four Paws Veterinary Center in Dublin, CA, is seeking an experienced and passionate Associate Veterinarian to join our growing two-doctor practice. Specializing in high-quality care for dogs and cats, we offer the perfect opportunity for someone eager to engage with a diverse caseload of medical, dental, and surgical cases.
 
Why Choose Four Paws Veterinary Center?
At Four Paws Veterinary Center, we are committed to providing top-notch veterinary care while fostering a supportive and inclusive workplace. With a strong emphasis on client education and comprehensive care, our clinic is designed to ensure the best outcomes for both pets and their families. Our location in Dublin, just 35 miles from San Francisco, offers the perfect blend of suburban living and easy access to a variety of outdoor and cultural activities. You can cool down at the Wave Waterpark, stroll through the Rose Garden at Emerald Glen Park or visit the museum at Dublin Heritage Park.  We are 42 miles to Half Moon Bay to enjoy a beach day with an adorable downtown or 56 miles to Napa where you can enjoy beautiful landscapes and wine tasting. Dublin offers a pleasant and enriching lifestyle with excellent weather, top-rated schools, and endless recreational opportunities.
 
What We Offer:
Competitive Compensation: $130,000-$200,000 per year + Production, with sign-on and relocation bonuses available.
Comprehensive Benefits Package: Including medical, dental, and vision insurance, as well as Short and Long Term Disability options.
Retirement Savings: 401K with company match.
Professional Development: Continuing Education allowance, AVMA Professional Liability Insurance, and VIN membership provided.
Work-Life Balance: No overnights or on-call shifts, and paid time off for vacation, sick days, and continuing education.
Additional Perks: Employee Assistance Program, team member discounts on pet care, and more.
Responsibilities:
Provide high-quality veterinary care, with a focus on medical, dental, and surgical procedures.
Build and maintain strong client relationships through exceptional communication and education.
Collaborate with our veterinary team in a positive and respectful environment.
Ensure timely and efficient case management with comprehensive medical records.
Educate clients on preventive care, nutrition, and overall pet health.
Who You Are:
A Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree holder.
Licensed to practice veterinary medicine in California, or eligible for licensure.
Passionate about engaging with a diverse caseload, including medical, dental, and surgical cases.
Committed to fostering client relationships and providing top-notch veterinary care.
Join Our Family
At Four Paws Veterinary Center, we are more than just a clinic—we are a family. As a proud United Veterinary Care Partner hospital, we celebrate the unique culture of each partner while benefiting from the support and resources that encourage fulfilling careers in veterinary medicine.
Join us at Four Paws Veterinary Center and contribute to making a positive impact on the lives of pets and their families while enjoying a balanced and rewarding career.
Apply today and be a part of our compassionate team!

Description
Position at Helping Hands Affordable Veterinary Surgery and Dental Care

Associate Veterinarian - Helping Hands Veterinary Surgery & Dentistry
Are you passionate about surgery and dentistry and looking for a role where you can truly make a difference? Helping Hands is seeking a compassionate, skilled, and enthusiastic Associate Veterinarian to join our mission-driven team.
At Helping Hands, we believe every pet deserves access to high-quality care. Our model focuses on providing exceptional surgical and dental services at an affordable cost—helping improve patient outcomes and reducing the heartbreaking need for economic euthanasia.
Why Join Helping Hands?
High Surgical & Dental Volume
Grow, refine, and expand your skill set with a diverse caseload and opportunities to learn new techniques.
Freedom to Practice Medicine
Our fixed-price structure eliminates financial pressure and allows you to focus purely on delivering the best care for each patient.
A True Work/Life Balance
Enjoy a Monday–Friday schedule with no required nights or weekends. Optional compensated emergency shifts are available—but never expected.
Flexibility That Fits Your Life
Full-time and part-time opportunities available
What You’ll Do
Confidently perform a wide range of soft tissue surgeries.
Interpret dental radiographs and perform multi-rooted extractions with proficiency.
Provide an exceptional level of care to every patient.
Build strong relationships with referring veterinarians and local partners.
Embody and champion the mission, values, and standards of Helping Hands.
Follow hospital policies and uphold high-quality medical recordkeeping.
Foster a collaborative, team-oriented environment.
What We’re Looking For
DVM or equivalent degree
A minimum of 2 years of post-graduate clinical experience
Active, in-good-standing state veterinary license
What We Offer
Competitive Compensation: $110,000–$150,000 per year
Comprehensive Benefits Package: Medical, dental, and vision insurance, plus options for Short-Term and Long-Term Disability
Retirement Savings: 401(k) with company match
Professional Growth: Generous Continuing Education Program, AVMA Professional Liability Insurance (PLIT), and VIN membership provided
Work–Life Balance: Paid time off—including sick, personal, vacation, and continuing education days
Additional Perks: Employee Assistance Program, team member pet-care discounts, and more

 

We are a proud United Veterinary Care Partner hospital. United Veterinary Care supports the unique culture and individuality of each partner hospital while empowering the hospital teams in their care for patients. One of our goals is to support our community of hospitals in creating sustainable, fulfilling careers in a profession we love.

Description
Position at Colchester Veterinary Hospital

Associate Veterinarian
Colchester, CT
 
Looking for a place where you can build lasting client relationships and get your adrenaline fix with urgent care cases? Colchester Veterinary Hospital has you covered. We’re a busy, full-service practice in beautiful Colchester, CT, and we’re ready to welcome our next veterinarian to the team.
 
Here’s what you’ll love about working here:
The Team: You won’t be flying solo. You’ll have the support of 9 experienced veterinarians plus a board-certified surgeon who makes complex procedures look easy.
The Balance: Wellness visits, urgent care, surgery—you’ll get variety without losing your mind to chaos.
The Mentorship: Whether you’re new to practice or just want to sharpen certain skills, we’ve got structured mentorship and plenty of colleagues who actually like sharing their knowledge.
The Location: When you’re off the clock, you’ve got everything from hiking at Day Pond State Park to kayaking on Lake Hayward to wineries, movie nights, family fun at the RecPlex, and, of course, frozen custard at Scotties. (Yes, you can bring us back some.)
What you’ll do here:
Provide high-quality care using the latest tools and tech.
Build strong, lasting relationships with our clients (and their furry, feathery, or scaly family members).
Keep things collaborative—our team works best when everyone’s in it together.
Stay organized with accurate records and treatment plans (SOAP notes, anyone?).
Help us keep up the positive, supportive vibe our clients and team rave about.
What we’re looking for:
A DVM (or soon-to-be) with a Connecticut license in good standing.
Someone who wants to contribute to a team-driven culture (bonus points if you like swapping funny pet stories during coffee breaks).
The Perks (because you deserve them):
Competitive pay and a benefits package stacked with the good stuff—medical, dental, vision, 401k with match, life insurance, CE support, professional dues covered, VIN access, and more.
PTO that actually lets you enjoy all those Colchester adventures.
Employee pet care discounts (because your pets deserve the best too).
Access to wellness programs, family-building support, and daily pay if you want it.
A workplace where your growth, your voice, and yes, your sense of humor, matter.
 
If you’re ready for a hospital where you can grow your skills, enjoy your work, and still have a life outside of it—let’s talk. Colchester Veterinary Hospital could be your next (and best) move.

column9: Firehouse Animal Health Centers Westlake is seeking a passionate Associate Veterinarian with a deep appreciation and commitment to veterinary medicine to join our team in Austin, TX
column14:
Description
Position at Firehouse Animal Health Center Westlake

Associate Veterinarian 
 
Join us at Firehouse Animal Health Centers, where 100 little things make a BIG difference! 
 
Firehouse Animal Health Centers Westlake is seeking a passionate Associate Veterinarian with a deep appreciation and commitment to veterinary medicine to join our team in Austin, TX.   

We take a team approach to veterinary medicine while maintaining at least a 2:1 Tech / Veterinarian ratio. We are Feline Friendly and uphold a Fear-Free environment for our patients and support training for all our team members. We believe in building relationships with our clients to improve our ability to provide the best care possible for our patients. We offer communication and leadership training to accomplish these goals. We apply a Diversity, Equity, and Inclusion lens in everything we do and love to give back to the community through donating veterinary services, helping with fundraisers, and volunteering our time to our community partners.
 
Essential Responsibilities & Functions:
Dedication to providing patients with a high level of veterinary care while utilizing the latest advances in diagnostics. 
A client service mindset with desire and ability to bring, build, and retain a strong client base. 
Enthusiasm about giving back to and building relationships with the local community. 
The ability to champion the practice’s objectives and values and inspire others. 
Follow the established policies and procedures that govern the normal operation of the hospital. 
Dedication to efficiently handling caseloads and maintaining accurate records using SOAP templates for treatment plans and recommendations. 
Promote a collaborative working environment and encourage the value of teamwork. 
Prioritize traumatic injuries and serious illnesses to ensure that all animals receive time-sensitive care. 
Educate clients on the importance of vaccinations, heartworm treatments, flea and tick treatments, and other aspects of preventive care. 
Requirements:
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree. 
State Veterinary Board License in good standing. 
Benefits and Perks: 
Paid time-off inclusive of personal, vacation, sick and CE
Medical, vision and dental insurance options
401K with company match 
Employer-paid short-term disability and life insurance 
Long-term disability option
Company-Sponsored Employee Assistance Program (Headspace)
Progyny-Fertility and Family Building Support 
Generous Continuing Education Program
Employee Discounts for Pet Care
AVMA Professional Liability Insurance  provided
VIN Membership
Reimbursement for: DEA and State Veterinary License, State VMA and AVMA dues
And More! 
Consider Relocating to Austin, Texas and Join Our Firehouse Team! 
 
The greater Austin area sits on the edge of "The Texas Hill Country,” and is a great destination for outdoor enthusiasts due to the hiking, mountain biking, climbing, road cycling, and water activities available at our many regional lakes. Many rivers and lakes in the area host water-based sports such as standup paddleboard, kayaking, canoeing, water skiing, tubing, wakeboarding, fishing, and rowing. The culinary and nightlife activities of The Hill Country have a little something for everyone and continue to grow. As the "Live Music Capital" of the world and there is music and dancing to be found every single night of the week.   Come see this awesome pet-friendly college town full of activities that support individuals and families.

Description
Position at Long Island Animal Hospital

Associate Veterinarian – Long Island Animal Hospital
📍 Long Island, NY | 2-Doctor Team | Must love animals, people, and short Saturdays
We don’t have a nap room or a hospital cat that does tricks… but we do have a great team and really good snacks.

We are a two-doctor hospital with no on-call shifts, no after-hours (or during the work day, for that matter!) drama, and a team that genuinely likes each other (and not just in a “nod in the hallway” kind of way).
 
We’re looking for an experienced Associate Veterinarian — someone with at least 2 years under their stethoscope — who can bring the skills, the empathy, and a sense of humor that pairs well with a busy treatment area and a fully stocked snack drawer.
Bonus points if you’re cool with working every other Saturday from 8:30 to 1:00. (Yes, just a crisp little half-day. You'll still make it to brunch, the beach, or your dog’s therapy appointment. We don't judge!)
You might be our dream vet if:
You’re confident in your medicine, collaborative in your approach, and can write SOAP notes without threatening your laptop.
You’ve got 2+ years of experience and still get excited about helping animals (even the ones with very spicy personalities).
You value your support staff and know that a good LVT is worth their weight in gold (we agree — and we’ve got them).
You believe "teamwork" isn’t just a buzzword — it’s the only way to survive a Monday.
You’re fluent in client communication, bad handwriting, and controlled chaos.
What you’ll actually do:
Practice medicine — full stop. Our incredible support team (including skilled LVTs) handles the rest so you can focus on what you do best.
See a mix of cases, build lasting client relationships, and actually enjoy your workday.
Work every other Saturday from 8:30 a.m. – 1:00 p.m. and then go live your weekend dreams.
Collaborate with a no-drama, all-in team that’s here to support you, laugh with you, and bring you coffee when needed.
What we bring to the table:
Salary range of $140,000 – 160,000 base + Production (no negative accrual — because we’re not monsters)
Absolutely no after-hours or on-call nonsense
Full benefits package: health, dental, vision, 401K with match, generous PTO, CE allowance, VIN, PLIT, licensing fees, and legit pet care discounts
Inclusive offerings like FOLX Healthcare for LGBTQIA+ team members
Headspace access, fertility/family-building support, DailyPay, and more
A leadership team that trusts you, respects your time, and understands that snacks = morale
We’re based in Long Island — the sweet spot between city energy and suburban chill. Think beaches, breweries, bagels, and actual time to enjoy them.
If you’re an experienced vet who’s ready to do your best work with a team that has your back — and your coffee order memorized — let's talk!

Can you picture yourself working in paradise and imagine feeling excited to come to work each day working alongside a team of genuinely good people who are dedicated to helping you succeed, be happy and grow both personally and professionally? What makes us different from other hospitals in the area is that we provide our team with a work environment that allows them to be their best.  This, in turn, enables us to dedicate the best experience to our clients and care for their pets, which is at the heart of our thriving practice.

Description
Position at New Hope Veterinary Hospital

Associate Veterinarian – New Hope Veterinary Hospital
(full or part time options available!)
Where your compassion meets community, and "doling out love” is part of the job.
 
New Hope Veterinary Hospital (NHVH) might just win the award for “Vet Clinic You’d Most Like to Bring Your Pet’s Holiday Card Photo Shoot To.” Nestled in charming New Hope, PA, NHVH has been serving local fur-families since 1969 and brings a refreshing mix of warmth, quality, and same-day care to every visit. We're known not just for our medical chops, but for treating both people and pets with exceptional, heartfelt service (and yes, we’ve been known to save the occasional stray in need).
 
Culture Vibes:
A relaxed yet professional environment where pets are family, care is personal, and compassion is the norm—not the exception.
Clients rave about how we handle emergencies swiftly, compassionately, and often more affordably than the big corporate clinics.
Same-day appointments keep things flexible and pet parents happy.
What You’ll Be Doing (and Loving Every Minute):
Practice full-spectrum, high-quality veterinary medicine—from routine wellness to diagnostics, surgery, dentistry, and emergencies.
Join an empathetic team that values continuity in patient care and builds trust over time.
Be part of an AAHA-accredited clinic (since 1978!), meaning you’ll practice under rigorous standards of care, pain management, and team training.
Use cutting-edge tools: in-house lab, imaging, diagnostics, and an online pharmacy for seamless care.
Thrive in a hospital that “meets you where you are,” offering same-day appointments, a welcoming vibe, and sincere client communication.
Who You Are (and Who We’d Be Honored to Have):
An experienced Veterinarian with passion and heart—someone ready to become part of the community, not just a job title.
A pro in general practice: medicine, surgery, dentistry, diagnostics—you’ve got range and you’re proud of it.
A compassionate communicator who can talk treatment plans in plain language, build trust, and make pet parents feel seen and heard.
Adaptable and enthusiastic—whether it’s a walk-in emergency or an unexpected mess in the lobby, you pivot with poise.
Why You’ll Stick Around (and Maybe Even Bring Your Pet’s Cousin):
A team-first, family-first vibe where you’re not just a coworker—you’re part of something bigger.
Consistent ability to deliver same-day care means less burnout, more impact.
Loving community with rave reviews, meaningful work, and positive feedback—especially when saving a stray or calming a jittery kitty fills your heart.
AAHA accreditation ensures you practice veterinary medicine the way good medicine should be practiced—thoroughly, ethically, expertly.
Ready to Join the New Hope Pack?
Send us your resume, say hello—and let’s get ready to offer top-tier care in a place that truly cares back. New Hope Veterinary Hospital is where leadership, laughter, and meaningful medicine collide. Your future patients—and their people—are waiting.

Elevating Services: Our bustling emergency hospital is scaling up with increasing caseloads in internal medicine and surgery. This is a prime opportunity for a dedicated Emergency Veterinarian eager to collaborate with a youthful and energetic group of specialists and ER doctors.

Our Culture: We take immense pride in nurturing a collegial atmosphere that upholds our CORE values, maintaining cohesion, collaboration, and a sense of fun even as we grow. Internship-trained or experienced emergency veterinarian.

Large 24/7 intensive care unit with highly trained technicians, criticalists, and experienced emergency clinicians.

Lead Veterinarian – St. Matthews Animal Clinic | Louisville, KY



Are you looking for that next step in your veterinary career? St. Matthews Animal Clinic located in Louisville, KY, is looking for a passionate Veterinarian with a deep appreciation and commitment for veterinary medicine to help lead our team. 



We are an AAHA-accredited, full-service, small animal practice with the ability to provide exceptional client service and high-quality medicine. We offer multiple medical services including digital radiography, a full in-house laboratory, ultrasound, dentistry, surgery, CO2 laser and class IV therapeutic laser, and even Stem Cell Therapy! In addition, we offer medical boarding and grooming which allows our hospital to service all of our client's needs. Our entire team is committed to providing an inclusive and supportive environment.



St. Matthews Animal Clinic is known as "The Clinic with a Big Heart” and it is our mission to ensure each patient and client entering our clinic experiences a connection in a way that feels like family. We are also passionate about giving back to the community and take pride in providing veterinary services to support our local shelters, help with fundraisers, and volunteer our time with area veterinary partners.

Flexible doctor schedules to support work-life balance

Generous Paid Time Off (PTO) including vacation, sick, personal, and CE days

Employer-provided short-term disability and life insurance

Referral bonus program & DailyPay on Demand

Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree. 

Active or ability to obtain state license (KY) and remain in good standing to practice

Strong surgical background 



About Louisville:

Open to relocating to Louisville, KY? This area offers a blend of Southern charm and vibrant city life. Known for its friendly community, the city boasts affordable living costs, making it an attractive option. Louisville is famed for the Kentucky Derby, embodying a rich horse racing tradition. The city's cultural scene is thriving, with numerous festivals, art galleries, and a diverse culinary landscape. Let’s not forget that it is known for its bourbon and many distilleries. Its strategic location provides easy access to various outdoor activities, such as hiking or walking across the pedestrian bridge and exploring the picturesque parks along the Ohio River. Louisville's strong job market and growing economy add to the appeal, making it a compelling destination for those seeking a welcoming community, diverse experiences, and career opportunities. Check out: Go to Louisville!

Are you passionate about emergency medicine and ready to make a meaningful impact? If you’re seeking part-time opportunities, CTVSEH invites you to join our dynamic team in the vibrant Austin area. We pride ourselves on delivering exceptional care through collaboration, innovation, and respect—for our patients, clients, and each other.
 
Why Choose CTVSEH?
 
Collaborative Expertise
Work shoulder-to-shoulder with board-certified specialists in critical care, internal medicine, dermatology, oncology, rehabilitation, neurology, ophthalmology, and surgery. Expand your surgical skills with hands-on, intraoperative training from our surgical team.
 
Flexibility That Fits Your Life
Your career should complement your lifestyle. We offer flexible scheduling options to help you achieve the balance you deserve.
 
Supportive Culture
We foster an environment of mutual respect and prioritize work-life balance, so you can thrive both professionally and personally.
 
What We’re Looking For
DVM/VMD degree
Prior mentorship or internship in emergency medicine (preferred)
 
Compensation & Benefits
Competitive Salary—No Non-Compete Agreements
Monthly Production Bonuses with no negative accrual
Comprehensive benefits package including:
401K with generous company match
AVMA PLIT coverage, VIN membership, and license/membership reimbursements
Paid time off for continuing education
CE allowance and access to leading veterinary publications
 
Why Austin?
Austin isn’t just a city—it’s a lifestyle. Known as the “Live Music Capital of the World,” Austin offers a vibrant cultural scene, incredible food, and endless outdoor adventures in its parks, lakes, and nearby Hill Country. Here, individuality is celebrated, and community thrives. If you’re looking for more than a job—if you want a place to belong—Austin is calling.
 
Ready to Join Us?
If you’re eager to practice exceptional medicine, collaborate with outstanding professionals, and enjoy life outside of work, we’d love to meet you!  Apply today via our career page or email your CV and cover letter to ann.schramm@unitedvetcare.com.
 
About Us
CTVSEH is proud to be a United Veterinary Care partner hospital. We celebrate the individuality of each hospital while providing resources and support to create sustainable, fulfilling careers in veterinary medicine.

Description
Position at Firehouse Belterra Village

Firehouse Animal Health Centers, where 100 little things make a BIG difference!          

Firehouse is looking for a part-time Veterinarian to join our awesome Belterra team! Curious about what sets Firehouse apart? Watch a short video featuring insights from a few of our exceptional Firehouse doctors.           

We take a team approach to veterinary medicine while maintaining at least a 2:1 Tech / Veterinarian ratio. We are Feline Friendly, uphold a Fear Free environment for our patients and support training for all our Team members too. We believe in building relationships with our clients to improve our ability to provide the best care possible for our patients. We offer communication and leadership training to accomplish these goals. We apply a Diversity, Equity, Inclusion lens in everything we do and love to give back to the community through donating veterinary services, helping with fundraisers, and volunteering our time to our community partners.   
      
Benefits and Perks  
Generous ProSal Compensation With NO Negative Accrual
CE / Professional Dues / Licenses
Personal Pet Care Discount
Veterinarian Mentorship Available – regardless of experience level
Qualifications   
DVM, VMD, or equivalent degree required
Licenses / registrations in compliance with local, state, and national requirements (must be authorized to work in the U.S. we do not provide VISA sponsorship)

Located right on the cusp of the “Texas Hill Country,” the greater Austin area practically shouts, “Adventure awaits, y’all!” Outdoor fanatics, get ready to be spoiled for a choice with hiking and biking. Rivers and lakes around here don’t just sit pretty—they’re the life of the party. From standup paddleboard pros to tubing enthusiasts and wakeboarding daredevils, there’s a water sport for every flavor of thrill-seeker. Now, let’s talk about the culinary scene—it’s not just food; it’s a flavor explosion! The nightlife? Well, it’s like the Hill Country decided to throw a never-ending fiesta. We’re not bragging, but we have something to satisfy every palate and keep the party going strong. And did we mention we’re the “Live Music Capital” of the world? Brace yourselves for nightly serenades and dance-worthy beats. Because around here, the music never stops, and the dancing shoes are always on standby. Bring your furry friends along because this place isn’t just awesome for humans. It’s a pet-friendly town that caters to everyone—from lone wolves to bustling families. 

For more information about Firehouse and our Veterinarian position, visit our website, https://firehouse.vet/careers. We hope to see you soon!

Description
Position at Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists

  Urgent Care Veterinarian    

At Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists, we're passionate about what we do and looking for like-minded individuals to join our Urgent Care team in our Dennis and Buzzards Bay, MA, locations.    
About This Role    
As an Urgent Care veterinarian, you'll play a crucial role on our team through outpatient receiving of non-critical, urgent cases that present through the ER service. Such cases will include, but are not limited to, bandage changes, ear infections, non-critical urinary issues, and acute lameness. Delivering high-quality medical care is essential, from presentation to discharge, where you can expect to utilize, hone, and increase skills and knowledge. This role may be perfect for you if you are a general practice veterinarian or a new graduate who enjoys urgent care medicine.    
    
You'll work with a team of skilled nursing staff, clinicians, and 22 specialists across eight specialties. We're looking for someone passionate about providing benevolent care and practicing comprehensive, quality medicine. Excellent written and verbal communication skills are important to excel in this role.    
    
At CCVS, we value education and offer well-established in-house technician training and continuing education programs, as well as Emergency and Critical Care internships, small animal rotating internships, and residency programs.  We also have an available mentorship program. Our central values of excellence, accountability, trust, respect, commitment, professionalism, integrity, innovation, collaboration, and compassion guide everything we do.    
Required Qualifications    
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine or equivalent; all state licensing requirements completed    
DEA license (state and federal)    
Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists' benefit package includes:    
Competitive annual salary ranging from $150,000 to $200,000, commensurate with experience, qualifications, and work schedule.    
Sign-on bonus
Relocation assistance is available    
Trupanion Pet Insurance with company paid premiums for two team member's pets    
Short Term Disability paid for by employer    
Weekly Grocery Delivery to the Hospital for meals/snacks    
Complimentary Life Insurance $25,000; with options to purchase additional voluntary benefits for self or family    
Company-Sponsored Employee Assistance Program    
401K with 100% company match up to 3% and 50% company match up to 5%, eligible after 6 months of employment    
United Healthcare Insurance Plans with multiple options    
Flexible Spending Account or Health Savings Account options    
United Healthcare Dental Insurance; includes a 100% paid premium option for team members    
United Healthcare Vision Insurance options    
Long Term Disability options    
Progyny-Fertility and Family Building Support (up to $10,000)    
Generous Continuing Education Program    
Uniforms (scrubs and jackets) provided at no cost to team members    
Team Member Discounts for Pet Care    
Paid PTO    
AVMA Professional Liability Insurance (PLIT) provided    
VIN provided    
Reimbursement for: MA Veterinary License / DEA License  / MVMA Dues / AVMA Dues/ VECCS membership    
Accounts with online publishers: Sage, Wiley, Elsevier    
About Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists    
Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists (CCVS) is a growing specialty and emergency-critical care hospital in Buzzards Bay and Dennis, MA. With more than 20 specialists across nine specialties, our highly skilled and compassionate team works to ensure pets receive quality, gold-standard medical care. As the only emergency and specialty referral hospital serving Cape Cod and the Islands, CCVS continues to grow to keep up with the needs of our community. We are looking for passionate and experienced veterinary professionals who welcome the challenge of meeting the highest standards for quality and performance while possessing a friendly and positive team-player approach.    
   
Imagine being part of a team that not only values your growth as a professional but also promotes it. At CCVS, investing in our employees' development is vital to achieving optimal patient care.    
    
So why not join us and become part of the Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists team? We're proud to be a United Veterinary Care Partner hospital, sponsoring sustainable, fulfilling careers for our team members while providing the best possible care to our community.

Description
Position at Sylvan Veterinary Hospital

Associate Veterinarian - Join Our Caring Team at Sylvan Veterinary Hospital, Modesto, CA 
 
Are you passionate about veterinary care and ready to embark on a rewarding journey? Sylvan Veterinary Hospital in Modesto, CA, is seeking an enthusiastic Associate Veterinarian to join our dedicated team. We welcome candidates of all experience levels, including new graduates, with a robust mentorship program available.
 
Why Choose Sylvan Veterinary Hospital? 
At Sylvan Veterinary Hospital, we believe in compassionate and quality care for our furry friends. As an integral part of our team, you'll be joining a well-established practice committed to providing exceptional veterinary services. Our hospital is equipped with modern facilities and a supportive team environment to ensure both our patients and staff thrive.
 
Living in Modesto, California, offers the perfect blend of urban amenities and small-town charm. Known for its rich agricultural heritage, Modesto provides an abundance of fresh, local produce and farmers' markets. The city boasts affordable housing, excellent schools, and various parks and recreational facilities, making it ideal for families. Centrally located, Modesto offers easy access to major California destinations like San Francisco, Sacramento, Lake Tahoe and Yosemite National Park. With a vibrant arts scene, diverse dining options, and annual events like the Graffiti Summer Festival, Modesto is a welcoming and lively place to call home.
 
New Graduates: 
At Sylvan Veterinary Hospital, we understand the transition from academia to practice can be challenging. That's why we're committed to providing a supportive environment for new graduates. Our experienced veterinarians are eager to mentor and guide you, offering invaluable insights and hands-on training. You'll have the opportunity to grow your skills and confidence as you embark on a fulfilling veterinary career.
 
What We Offer: 
Competitive Compensation: $110,000-$180,000 per year + Production Bonus and Sign-on Bonus.
Comprehensive Benefits Package: Including medical, dental, and vision insurance, as well as options for Short Term and Long Term Disability.
Retirement Savings: 401K with company match.
Professional Growth: Generous Continuing Education Program, AVMA Professional Liability Insurance (PLIT), and VIN membership provided.
Work-Life Balance: Paid time off inclusive of sick, personal, vacation, and continuing education days.
Additional Perks: Employee Assistance Program, team member discounts for pet care, and more.
Responsibilities: 
Deliver high-quality veterinary care, utilizing advanced diagnostic tools and techniques.
Develop and maintain a strong client base, offering excellent customer service.
Foster relationships within the community, contributing to our hospital's positive reputation.
Uphold and advocate for the values and objectives of Sylvan Veterinary Hospital.
Efficiently manage caseloads, ensuring timely care and maintaining comprehensive medical records.
Prioritize urgent cases and provide necessary care promptly.
Educate clients on preventive care, nutrition, and overall pet health.
Who You Are: 
A Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree holder.
Licensed to practice veterinary medicine in California, or eligible for licensure.
Join Our Family: 
At Sylvan Veterinary Hospital, we are more than just a workplace; we are a close-knit family dedicated to making a difference in the lives of pets and their owners. Our hospital serves as a cornerstone of the Modesto community, providing a wide range of wellness, surgical, and dental care services.
Embark on a career where your passion for animal healthcare is nurtured and valued. Enjoy the support of experienced veterinarians, opportunities for professional growth, and a fulfilling work environment.

Description
Position at Bay State Veterinary Emergency and Specialty Services

Exciting Opportunity for a Veterinary Cardiologist at BSVESS!
 
Bay State Veterinary Emergency and Specialty Services (BSVESS) in Swansea, MA, is thrilled to offer a stellar opportunity for a Veterinary Cardiologist with a passion for excellence. Join us and play a pivotal role in our thriving specialty practice!
 
About BSVESS: Since 2006, BSVESS has been a cornerstone of 24/7 Emergency Care, Internal Medicine, and Surgery. In summer 2024, we moved to a newly renovated 36,000-sf facility, complete with advanced internal medicine, surgery services, and a state-of-the-art radiation oncology center. We’re also expanding our services to include oncology, ophthalmology, cardiology, and dermatology.
 
Our Culture: At BSVESS, we cherish our close-knit atmosphere, fostering a supportive and collegial environment amid our growth. Our team stands as a beacon of exceptional medicine, mutual respect, and a positive, cooperative ethos.
 
The Opportunity: BSVESS is seeking a passionate and skilled Veterinary Cardiologist eager to join our expanding practice. If you are a team player dedicated to excellence in veterinary medicine and fostering a supportive team environment, this is your chance to excel and make a significant impact. Please apply online!
 
Requirements:
Diplomate of ACVIM (Cardiology) or residency trained, eligible to sit for the certifying exam.
Eligible to obtain a veterinary license to practice in Massachusetts.
Facility Highlights:
Large 24/7 intensive care unit with highly trained technicians, criticalists, and experienced emergency clinicians.
Cutting-edge equipment: digital radiography, ultrasound, CT, endoscopy, and a linear accelerator.
Investment in cardiology equipment as needed.
Privileges at our sister hospital (Ocean State Veterinary Specialists) for fluoroscopy and surgical procedures.
Ample office space for a quiet and focused environment.
Benefits:
Complimentary Short Term Disability.
Complimentary Life Insurance with options to purchase additional voluntary benefits for self or family.
Company Sponsored Employee Assistance Program (Headspace).
401K with 100% company match up to 3% and 50% company match up to 5%, eligible after 6 months of employment.
United Health Care UMR Insurance Plans with 3 tiered options; Kaiser for CA Residents.
Flexible Spending Account or Health Savings Account options.
United Healthcare Dental Insurance, including a 100% paid premium option for team members.
United Healthcare Vision Insurance options.
Long Term Disability option (covered by employee).
Progyny-Fertility and Family Building Support.
Generous Continuing Education Program.
Team Member Discounts for Pet Care.
Paid time off inclusive of sick, personal, vacation, and CE.
AVMA Professional Liability Insurance (PLIT) provided.
VIN provided.
Reimbursement for state Veterinary License, DEA License, state VMA Dues, AVMA Dues, and specialty dues.
Master accounts with online publishers: Sage, Wiley, Elsevier.
LGBTQIA+ support with FOLX HEALTHCARE (if UMR enrolled).
Sword Digital Musculoskeletal & Physical Therapy Program (if UMR enrolled).
Referral Bonus Program.
DailyPay on Demand.
Base salary ranges: $200k - $260k (commensurate with experience) plus eligible for quarterly production bonuses.
Signing and relocation bonuses available.
Location: Conveniently located in Swansea, MA, just 15 minutes from Providence, RI, BSVESS offers the opportunity to live in a variety of wonderful areas, including urban, rural, and beach communities. Easy access to Cape Cod and the Greater Boston area provides a diverse range of living options. BSVESS is a proud United Veterinary Care partner hospital.
 
Join Us: United Veterinary Care supports the unique culture and individuality of each partner hospital while empowering the hospital teams in their care for patients. Our goal is to support our community of hospitals in creating sustainable, fulfilling careers in a profession we love. There are no corporate pressures on the specialists - we just practice excellent medicine and strive to do right by our clients and patients.
 
Equal Employment Opportunity United Veterinary Care provides equal employment opportunities to all employees and applicants without regard to race, color, religion, age, mental disability, physical disability, medical condition, gender, sexual orientation, genetic information, ancestry, marital status, national origin, veteran status, and other classifications protected by applicable state and local non-discrimination laws.
 
Apply Today! Join us at BSVESS and become part of a team dedicated to providing exceptional veterinary care with a personal touch. Let's advance veterinary care together!

Position at Solano-Napa Pet Emergency Clinic

Join Our Team as a Veterinary Cardiologist at Solano-Napa Pet Specialty Clinic (SPEC) in Fairfield, CA  
  
Are you a diplomate or practice limited to small animal cardiologist seeking a dynamic and diverse practice environment? Solano-Napa Pet Specialty Clinic (SPEC) is a well-equipped 24/7 hospital strategically located near San Francisco, Napa Valley, Davis, and Sacramento. Having served the area for 20 years with a strong client base, SPEC is excited to offer an opportunity for a Veterinary Cardiologist to join our team! Our commitment to excellence has earned us the trust and loyalty of pet owners in the community. With the addition of specialty services in the summer of 2026, we are poised to continue our tradition of providing top-notch care.  
  
Why Work with Us?  
  
Fun and Fulfilling Environment Enjoy a drama-free, happy staff and a supportive atmosphere that significantly boosts job satisfaction and overall well-being. Our positive culture enhances employee morale and the quality of care provided to pets in need. Be part of our practice as we grow into a new space with added specialties in the summer of 2026.  
  
Collaborative Teamwork Work closely with Veterinarians, Registered Veterinary Technicians (RVTs), and assistants. Our team shares shifts, collaborates, exchanges ideas, and supports each other during challenging times, fostering a strong team dynamic.  
  
Growth and Development We are committed to continuous learning and skill improvement. As a growing practice, SPEC offers ample opportunities for professional development in a dynamic and forward-thinking environment.  
  
Advanced Diagnostic Opportunities Enjoy the opportunity to perform advanced diagnostic procedures with onsite CT and endoscopy equipment.  
  
Compensation and Benefits  
This opportunity offers a comprehensive package of financial and wellness benefits, including potential annual of $275,000 base plus production, extensive healthcare options through United Health Care UMR and Kaiser (for CA residents), 401K matching, complimentary life and short-term disability insurance, and a host of other voluntary benefits. Additional perks include professional development support, team member discounts for pet care, various insurance options, and unique programs like Progyny for fertility support and FOLX healthcare for LGBTQIA+ support. Flexible spending accounts, paid time off, employee assistance programs, and daily pay options further enhance the overall offer. Plus, it covers professional dues and licenses, providing a holistic support system for employees' professional and personal well-being.  
  
Location Perks  
Located near Napa Wine Country and San Francisco, SPEC offers a unique combination of cultural events and outdoor activities for employees to enjoy during their time off. This vibrant and diverse lifestyle is especially appealing.  
  
United Veterinary Care Partner  
We are a proud United Veterinary Care Partner hospital. United Veterinary Care supports the unique culture and individuality of each partner hospital while empowering the hospital teams in their care for patients. One of our goals is to support our community of hospitals in creating sustainable, fulfilling careers in a profession we love.

About Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists

Description
Position at Ocean State Veterinary Specialists

Join the Ocean State Veterinary Specialists (OSVS) Team in East Greenwich, RI!  
  
Location: East Greenwich, RI  
  
Are You a Board-Certified or Residency-Trained Veterinary Criticalist? OSVS is expanding our dynamic team, and we want you to be a part of our growth!  
 
Why Choose OSVS?  
Teaching opportunities: Our team of specialists are committed to teaching the next generation of veterinary professionals with our house officer programs.
State-of-the-Art Facility: Our 36,000 sq. ft. facility offers comprehensive referral and emergency services.  
Brand New Dedicated ICU: Designed for efficiency, our ICU provides ample space for our medical team, including separate isolation wards for respiratory and GI cases, oxygen cages, quiet runs, and numerous cages and kennels.  
Advanced Technology: Our facility is equipped with cutting-edge tools such as endoscopy, digital radiography, ultrasound, CT, MRI, laparoscopy, fluoroscopy, mechanical ventilation, telemetry, and state-of-the-art monitoring equipment.  
Collaborative Network: We are partnered with Bay State Veterinary Emergency and Specialty Services (BSVESS), enhancing patient care across RI, eastern CT, and southern MA.  
Our Culture
We nurture a collegial atmosphere that upholds our CORE values of cohesion, collaboration, and fun. Our commitment is to assemble a team that embodies excellence in veterinary medicine, mutual respect, and a cooperative spirit.  
  
Requirements  
Diplomate of ACVR or Residency-Trained Veterinary Radiologist, eligible to sit for the certifying exam.  
Eligible to obtain a veterinary license to practice in Rhode Island.  
Facility Highlights  
Large 24/7 intensive care unit with highly trained technicians, boarded specialists, and experienced emergency clinicians.  
Cutting-edge equipment: digital radiography, ultrasound, CT, MRI, endoscopy, laparoscopy, fluoroscopy, mechanical ventilation, telemetry, and state-of-the-art monitoring equipment.  
Ample office space for focused work on calls and paperwork.  
Benefits  
At Ocean State Veterinary Specialists (OSVS), we offer a comprehensive benefits package to support our team members. Benefits include complimentary short-term disability and life insurance, a company-sponsored employee assistance program (Headspace), a 401K plan with a company match, United Health Care UMR insurance plans with dental and vision options, long term disability, and Progyny-Fertility and Family Building Support. We also provide a generous continuing education program, pet care discounts, paid time off, professional liability insurance (PLIT), and VIN membership. Additional perks include reimbursements for licenses and dues, master accounts with online publishers, LGBTQIA+ support with FOLX HEALTHCARE, access to Sword Digital Musculoskeletal & Physical Therapy Program, a referral bonus program, and DailyPay on Demand. Join OSVS to advance your veterinary career with extensive benefits and support! 🐾  
 
Location  
OSVS in East Greenwich, RI, is a large and busy 24-hour emergency and specialty referral center, providing comprehensive care to the surrounding areas. Conveniently located just 15 minutes from Providence, RI, offering access to urban, rural, and beach communities. Easy access to Cape Cod and the Greater Boston area provides a diverse range of living options.  
  
We are a proud United Veterinary Care Partner hospital. Position at Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists

Veterinary Dentist
Location: Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists – Buzzards Bay, MA
 
Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists (CCVS) is excited to announce the expansion of our specialty services to include Veterinary Dentistry in our Buzzards Bay location in 2027. We are currently seeking a board-certified Veterinary Dentist or residency-trained veterinarian to join our growing team of specialists and help us build a high-quality dentistry service within our well-established emergency and referral hospitals.
 
Who We Are:

CCVS is a busy, multi-specialty referral and emergency hospital serving pets throughout Cape Cod and southeastern Massachusetts. With locations in Buzzards Bay and Dennis, our team includes specialists in the established disciplines of emergency & critical care, internal medicine, surgery, cardiology, dermatology, exotics, anesthesiology, and diagnostic imaging. With our expansion, we’re building to include dentistry, ophthalmology, and neurology. Our collaborative hospital is the perfect environment for excellent patient outcomes and your professional growth and development.
 
What You’ll Do:
 
Provide expert diagnosis and treatment for a wide range of dental conditions
Collaborate with other specialists and emergency clinicians in a team-based care model
Help establish and grow the dentistry service from the ground up
Support the education of interns, residents, and veterinary staff
Contribute to a culture of compassionate, high-quality care

Diplomate of the AVDC or dentistry residency-completed veterinarian eligible for board certification by 2027
A motivated, team-oriented individual with a strong commitment to patient care and client communication
An interest in mentoring and professional development of others
Passion for building something new in a supportive, forward-thinking hospital environment
What We Offer:
 
Competitive salary range from $165 to $185/k base plus production, with sign-on and relocation support
Comprehensive benefits including health, dental, 401(k), CE allowance, and paid time off
Supportive culture that values innovation, collaboration, and personal well-being
Access to state-of-the-art equipment and facilities, with plans for a dedicated dentistry department
Be part of something special! Join CCVS as we expand our services and continue to deliver exceptional veterinary care to our community.
 
Apply now to be part of our 2027 launch, or contact us directly at recruiting@capecodvetspecialists.com to learn more about this exciting opportunity.

Veterinary Internal Medicine Specialist (Small Animal)  
Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists is seeking a Veterinary Small Animal Internal Medicine Specialist with board certification or residency training to join our team of specialists and associates.  
About this Role  
Veterinarians specializing in internal medicine are often called "puzzle solvers." At CCVS, you will use those problem-solving skills to support your patients, their families, and colleagues. This includes working closely with referring veterinarians, providing comprehensive care for chronic cases, and offering second opinions on complex cases. You will also provide specialized hospitalization and therapies when necessary and perform complex diagnostic procedures such as endoscopy or bronchoscopy.   
 If you're a passionate veterinary internist, we offer an exciting opportunity for you to work with a team of skilled nursing staff, clinicians, and 22 specialists across 8 specialties. Our facility is dedicated to providing compassionate care and practicing comprehensive, quality medicine for our patients and clients. We highly value excellent written and verbal communication skills to ensure effective collaboration and success in this role.  
At our facility, we prioritize education and offer a range of well-established in-house technician training, CE programs, and internships and residency programs. Our central values of excellence, accountability, trust, respect, commitment, professionalism, integrity, innovation, collaboration, and compassion are at the core of everything we do.  
You'll enjoy a four-day/week schedule, generous compensation, and a sign-on bonus. If needed, we offer relocation assistance. This role is scheduled in both Buzzards Bay and Dennis locations, making it a convenient and flexible opportunity. 
 Required Qualifications  
Doctor of Veterinary Medicine degree or equivalent; all state licensing requirements completed   
DEA license (state and federal)  
Residency-trained   
Board-certified or board-eligible  
Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists benefit package includes:  
Competitive annual salary ranging from $200,000 to $225,000, plus production, commensurate with experience, qualifications, and work schedule.
Trupanion Pet Insurance with company paid premiums for two team member's pets  
Short Term Disability paid for by employer

Accounts with online publishers: Sage, Wiley, Elsevier  
About Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists  
Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists (CCVS) is a growing specialty and emergency-critical care hospital in Buzzards Bay and Dennis, MA. With more than 20 specialists across nine specialties, our highly skilled and compassionate team works to ensure pets receive quality, gold-standard medical care. As the only emergency and specialty referral hospital serving Cape Cod and the Islands, CCVS continues to grow to keep up with the needs of our community. We are looking for enthusiastic and experienced veterinary professionals who welcome the challenge of meeting the highest standards for quality and performance while possessing a friendly and positive team-player approach.  
Imagine being part of a team that not only values your growth as a professional but also supports it. At CCVS, investing in our employees' development is vital to achieving optimal patient care.

Description
Position at Animal Clinic East





Animal Clinic Inc. is seeking a Medical Director to help lead our two hospital locations (Animal Clinic East & Animal Clinic Downtown) in Chattanooga, TN. 

Founded in 1953, Animal Clinic Inc. of Chattanooga has been dedicated for more than 60 years to providing exemplary services that consistently exceed the expectations of our clients and their highly valued pets. We are a full service veterinary medical facility with two full-service clinic locations in Chattanooga, TN. The professional and courteous staff at Animal Clinic Downtown & Animal Clinic East seek to provide the best possible medical, surgical, and dental care for our highly valued patients.  We go above and beyond to meet the strict requirements of AAHA, The American Animal Hospital Association, to maintain our accreditation for caring for companion animals. AAHA accreditation is sought voluntarily by only 15 percent of North American veterinary practices, and we are proud to be one of them!   

 
Position Overview

The primary purpose and function of the Medical Director is to lead and manage the hospital and ensure the highest quality of medical care, unmatched client experience, associate engagement, and maximum productivity of the hospital team. The Medical Director advocates for the pets and their quality of life; builds a long-term relationship with the team and clients and educates clients and hospital team on all aspects of pet health and the human, pet bond. The Medical Director partners with the Operations Advisor, support team, and Practice Manger to operate an effective and productive hospital team, ensure a safe and engaging hospital environment, and improve the medical quality, client experience and business performance of the hospital.



Leadership Responsibilities

Oversee and train the team on professional, efficient, and unmatched client experience (lead by example), to include client education, preventive care, pet health needs, marketing campaigns, and effectively resolve client issues.

Partner with the Operations Advisor, Support Center and Practice Manager to interview, select, train, develop, coach, mentor, and if necessary, discipline and terminate team members.

Work with the Practice Manager to develop development plans for associate doctors to ensure training and development to deliver the Vision and Mission of UVC.

Partnering with the Practice Manager, effectively create and maintain veterinarians’ work schedules to ensure proper coverage to meet client needs and proactively plan PTO and CE scheduling.

Develop annual planning, budgeting, and hospital productivity levels for the hospitals in partnership with Support team, OA and hospital leadership.

Provide effective communication between the hospital team, clients, PM,


Functional Responsibilities

Establish trust and gain the confidence and compliance of clients through effectively delivering appropriate preventive care, performing complete diagnostic work ups, developing thorough treatment plans, communicating home care instructions, and planning follow up visits.

Effectively communicate diagnosis and treatment plan to hospital team and clients

Build collaborative relationships: build strong professional relationships with UVC team members, clients, and community.

Lead the hospital team through the daily schedule and caseload, delegating tasks to the team and improving hospital efficiency.

Work with the Practice Manager to Ensure that all client concerns are addressed and resolved in a timely manner.



Qualifications/Requirements:

Doctor of Veterinary Medicine (DVM) degree.  

TN Veterinary License in good standing or eligibility for licensure with the applicable state veterinary board.

4+ years as a veterinarian with leadership experience  



Benefits:  

Complimentary Short-Term Disability

Complimentary Life Insurance $25,000; with options to purchase additional voluntary benefits for self or family 

Company Sponsored Employee Assistance Program (Headspace)

401K with 100% company match up to 3% and 50% company match up to 5%, eligible after 6 months of employment

Medical Insurance Plans with 3 tiered options

Flexible Spending Account or Health Savings Account options 

United Healthcare Dental Insurance; includes a 100% paid premium option for team members

United Healthcare Vision Insurance options

Long Term Disability option (covered by employee)

Generous Continuing Education benefits

Team Member Discounts for Pet Care

Generous Paid time Off (PTO)

AVMA Professional Liability Insurance (PLIT) provided

VIN provided

Reimbursement for: state Veterinary License / DEA License / state VMA Dues / AVMA Dues/ +/-specialty dues 

Master accounts with online publishers: Sage, Wiley, Elsevier

Referral Bonus Program

DailyPay on Demand





About Us: 

United Veterinary Care takes pride in embracing the uniqueness and diversity that every team member, pet owner, and pet brings to our community.  Our unwavering dedication revolves around nurturing a culture of diversity, equity, and inclusion where each team member not only feels a strong sense of belonging but is also empowered to thrive.

We are a proud United Veterinary Care Partner hospital. United Veterinary Care supports the unique culture and individuality of each partner hospital while empowering the hospital teams in their care for patients. One of our goals is to support our community of hospitals in creating sustainable, fulfilling careers in a profession we love.

Are you a passionate veterinarian ready to lead, inspire, and elevate the future of patient care? Crestwood Animal Hospital is looking for a forward-thinking Medical Director to guide our thriving, AAHA-accredited practice and shape the next chapter of veterinary excellence in the Louisville area.
Lead With Purpose. Practice With Pride.

You’ll champion high-quality medicine, mentor a talented team, and influence the strategic direction of a hospital that is deeply committed to its patients, clients, and community.

Oversee daily hospital operations, delegate effectively, and uphold high standards of care.

Model ethical decision-making and elevate medical quality across the hospital.

We lean into technology, prioritize patient comfort through our Fear-Free and Feline-Friendly certifications, and foster a collaborative, upbeat culture where every team member’s voice matters. AVMA PLIT coverage

Employee pet-care discounts
…and more!

UVC is an Equal Opportunity Employer, committed to a workplace where every individual is respected and valued.

Description
Position at Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists

Veterinary Neurologist
Location: Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists – Buzzards Bay, MA
 
Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists (CCVS) is excited to announce the expansion of our specialty services to include Veterinary Neurology in our Buzzards Bay location in 2027. We are currently seeking a board-certified Veterinary Neurologist or residency-trained veterinarian to join our growing team of specialists and help us build a high-quality neurology service within our well-established emergency and referral hospitals.
 
Who We Are:
CCVS is a busy, multi-specialty referral and emergency hospital serving pets throughout Cape Cod and southeastern Massachusetts. With locations in Buzzards Bay and Dennis, our team includes specialists in the established disciplines of emergency & critical care, internal medicine, surgery, cardiology, dermatology, exotics, anesthesiology, and diagnostic imaging. With our expansion, we’re building to include dentistry, ophthalmology, and neurology. Our collaborative hospital is the perfect environment for excellent patient outcomes and your professional growth and development.
 
What You’ll Do:
Provide expert diagnosis and treatment for a wide range of neurological conditions
Collaborate with other specialists and emergency clinicians in a team-based care model
Help establish and grow the neurology service from the ground up
Support the education of interns, residents, and veterinary staff
Contribute to a culture of compassionate, high-quality care
What We’re Looking For:
Diplomate of the ACVIM (Neuro) or neurology residency-completed veterinarian eligible for board certification by 2027
A motivated, team-oriented individual with a strong commitment to patient care and client communication
An interest in mentoring and the professional development of others
Passion for building something new in a supportive, forward-thinking hospital environment
What We Offer:
Competitive salary range from $200k to $250K annually plus production, with sign-on and relocation support
Comprehensive benefits including health, dental, 401(k), CE allowance, and paid time off
Supportive culture that values innovation, collaboration, and personal well-being
Access to state-of-the-art equipment and facilities, with plans for a dedicated neurology suite
Be part of something special! Join CCVS as we expand our services and continue to deliver exceptional veterinary care to our community.
 
Apply now to be part of our 2027 launch, or contact us directly at recruiting@capecodvetspecialists.com to learn more about this exciting opportunity.

Description
Position at Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists

Veterinary Radiologist
 
Location: Buzzards Bay, MA
Full-Time | Board-Certified or Board-eligible 
 
Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists (CCVS) is seeking an on-site Veterinary Radiologist to join our growing team at our 24/7 Emergency and Specialty Hospital in Buzzards Bay, MA. If you're looking for a career where your expertise is valued, your well-being is prioritized, and you can make a meaningful impact on patient care, we'd love to meet you!
 
Why Choose CCVS?
 
CCVS is a growing specialty and emergency-critical care hospital with two locations, Buzzards Bay and Dennis, MA. With more than 20 specialists across nine specialties, our highly skilled and compassionate team works to ensure pets receive quality, gold-standard medical care.
 
At CCVS, we are more than just a hospital—we are a community of passionate veterinary professionals dedicated to lifelong learning and advancing veterinary medicine. As the only emergency and referral hospital serving Cape Cod and the Islands, our radiologists play a critical role in supporting our team of specialists, emergency doctors, and referring veterinarians.
 
Your Role & Impact
 
Utilize state-of-the-art diagnostic tools to provide expert imaging interpretation
Collaborate closely with specialists in every department
Mentor and support our interns, residents, and dedicated veterinary technicians
Work alongside a skilled support team, including doctor assistants and client care professionals, allowing you to focus on your specialty
Engage in continuous learning and professional development, both as a mentor and a lifelong student
Enjoy work-life balance with a flexible four-day workweek
Advanced Imaging & Equipment
Our diagnostic imaging service is equipped with:
 
Sound Smart PACS & VetRocket reporting system
Dragon voice recognition software for efficient reporting
new in-house 64-slice GE Revolution Maxima CT
GE Logiq E10 and Logiq S8 ultrasound machines
Exciting upgrades are coming as we continue to expand our hospital and services!
A Culture of Learning & Growth
 
At CCVS, we believe in investing in our people and fostering a culture of continuous learning. Whether through formal continuing education, mentorship, case collaboration, or hands-on training, we encourage professional growth at every level. You’ll have the opportunity to:
Teach and mentor veterinary interns, residents, and technicians
Participate in and lead rounds, case discussions, and CE sessions
Stay at the forefront of radiology advancements with access to the latest tools and resources
Collaborate with specialists across disciplines to enhance patient outcomes
What We’re Looking For:
 
Board-certified or board-eligible Veterinary Radiologist (DACVR) in-hospital
Strong communication skills and a passion for teamwork
Enthusiasm for collaboration, mentorship, and education
Commitment to excellence in patient care and ongoing learning
Our Culture & Values
 
At CCVS, we cultivate a supportive and inclusive environment where team members feel valued and inspired. We believe in:
Work-life balance – Flexible schedules that support your professional and personal well-being
Medical excellence – Cutting-edge diagnostics and gold-standard patient care
Collaboration – A team-first approach to veterinary medicine
Education & growth – Opportunities for mentorship, leadership, and lifelong learning
Diversity & inclusion – A workplace where everyone is respected and empowered
Comprehensive Benefits Package:
 
Competitive salary abse from $200k to $300k annually,  + production, for on-site radiologist
Relocation assistance available
401K with company match
Medical, dental, and vision insurance options
Trupanion pet insurance (company-paid for two pets!)
Generous CE allowance + reimbursement for specialty dues & licensing
AVMA PLIT professional liability insurance covered 
Paid time off & paid holidays
Uniforms (scrubs & jackets) provided
Team member discounts on pet care
Weekly grocery deliveries for meals/snacks at the hospital
Employer-paid short-term disability & life insurance
Employee Assistance Program (EAP) 
Why Cape Cod?
 
With 560 miles of stunning coastline, Cape Cod is a dream destination for those who love the ocean, outdoor adventures, and small-town charm. Whether you’re exploring the Cape Cod National Seashore, enjoying fresh seafood, or taking a quick trip to Boston or Providence, you’ll find the perfect balance between career fulfillment and personal relaxation.
 
If You’re Ready to Make a Difference
 
Why not join us and become part of the Cape Cod Veterinary Specialists team? We're proud to be a United Veterinary Care Partner hospital, supporting sustainable, fulfilling careers for our team members while providing the best possible care to our community. If you’re a Veterinary Radiologist looking for a collaborative, learning-focused environment in a beautiful coastal setting, we’d love to hear from you!
 
Apply today at https://capecodvetspecialists.com/careers/ or email your CV and cover letter to recruiting@capecodvetspecialists.com.

Description
Position at Ocean State Veterinary Specialists

Join the Ocean State Veterinary Specialists (OSVS) Team in East Greenwich, RI!  
  
Location: East Greenwich, RI  
  
Are You a Board-Certified or Residency-Trained Veterinary Radiologist? OSVS is expanding our dynamic team, and we want you to be a part of our growth!  
 
Why Choose OSVS?  
Expanding Services: Our hospital is experiencing increasing caseloads in internal medicine and surgery, offering a rewarding career for dedicated professionals.  
State-of-the-Art Facility: Our 36,000 sq. ft. facility offers comprehensive referral and emergency services.  
Brand New Dedicated ICU: Designed for efficiency, our ICU provides ample space for our medical team, including separate isolation wards for respiratory and GI cases, oxygen cages, quiet runs, and numerous cages and kennels.  
Advanced Technology: Our facility is equipped with cutting-edge tools such as endoscopy, digital radiography, ultrasound, CT, MRI, laparoscopy, fluoroscopy, mechanical ventilation, telemetry, and state-of-the-art monitoring equipment.  
Collaborative Network: We are partnered with Bay State Veterinary Emergency and Specialty Services (BSVESS), enhancing patient care across RI, eastern CT, and southern MA.  
Our Culture
We nurture a collegial atmosphere that upholds our CORE values of cohesion, collaboration, and fun. Our commitment is to assemble a team that embodies excellence in veterinary medicine, mutual respect, and a cooperative spirit.  
  
Requirements  
Diplomate of ACVR or Residency-Trained Veterinary Radiologist, eligible to sit for the certifying exam.  
Eligible to obtain a veterinary license to practice in Rhode Island.  
Facility Highlights  
Large 24/7 intensive care unit with highly trained technicians, boarded specialists, and experienced emergency clinicians.  
Cutting-edge equipment: digital radiography, ultrasound, CT, MRI, endoscopy, laparoscopy, fluoroscopy, mechanical ventilation, telemetry, and state-of-the-art monitoring equipment.  
Ample office space for focused work on calls and paperwork.  
Benefits  
At Ocean State Veterinary Specialists (OSVS), we offer a comprehensive benefits package to support our team members. Benefits include complimentary short-term disability and life insurance, a company-sponsored employee assistance program (Headspace), a 401K plan with a company match, United Health Care UMR insurance plans with dental and vision options, long term disability, and Progyny-Fertility and Family Building Support. We also provide a generous continuing education program, pet care discounts, paid time off, professional liability insurance (PLIT), and VIN membership. Additional perks include reimbursements for licenses and dues, master accounts with online publishers, LGBTQIA+ support with FOLX HEALTHCARE, access to Sword Digital Musculoskeletal & Physical Therapy Program, a referral bonus program, and DailyPay on Demand. Join OSVS to advance your veterinary career with extensive benefits and support! 🐾  
 
Location  
OSVS in East Greenwich, RI, is a large and busy 24-hour emergency and specialty referral center, providing comprehensive care to the surrounding areas. Conveniently located just 15 minutes from Providence, RI, offering access to urban, rural, and beach communities. Easy access to Cape Cod and the Greater Boston area provides a diverse range of living options.  
  
We are a proud United Veterinary Care Partner hospital. United Veterinary Care supports the unique culture and individuality of each partner hospital while empowering the hospital teams in their care for patients. One of our goals is to support our community of hospitals in creating sustainable, fulfilling careers in a profession we love.

Are you a diplomate or practice limited to small animal radiologist seeking a dynamic and diverse practice environment? Solano-Napa Pet Specialty Clinic (SPEC) is a well-equipped 24/7 hospital strategically located near San Francisco, Napa Valley, Davis, and Sacramento. Having served the area for 20 years with a strong client base, SPEC is excited to offer an opportunity for a Veterinary Radiologist to join our team! With the addition of specialty services in the summer of 2026, we are poised to continue our tradition of providing top-notch care.

Flexible spending accounts, paid time off, employee assistance programs, and daily pay options further enhance the overall offer. About This Role    
As an Urgent Care veterinarian, you'll play a crucial role on our team through outpatient receiving of non-critical, urgent cases that present through the ER service. Such cases will include, but are not limited to, bandage changes, ear infections, non-critical urinary issues, and acute lameness. Delivering high-quality medical care is essential, from presentation to discharge, where you can expect to utilize, hone, and increase skills and knowledge. This role may be perfect for you if you are a general practice veterinarian or a new graduate who enjoys urgent care medicine.    
    
You'll work with a team of skilled nursing staff, clinicians, and 22 specialists across eight specialties. We're looking for someone passionate about providing benevolent care and practicing comprehensive, quality medicine. Excellent written and verbal communication skills are important to excel in this role.    
    
At CCVS, we value education and offer well-established in-house technician training and continuing education programs, as well as Emergency and Critical Care internships, small animal rotating internships, and residency programs.  We also have an available mentorship program. Our central values of excellence, accountability, trust, respect, commitment, professionalism, integrity, innovation, collaboration, and compassion guide everything we do.
